ref: Then he said to the servant in charge of the first herd, “When Esau meets you, he will ask whose servant you are. He will want to know where you are going and who owns those animals in front of you.
text: Wa yaa weeni waai le dɛ niŋ la ayen, <<Ase m suoku Iisɔ a dan tu fu suik a bek fu ayen, <Ka wana ale soa fi, fi be a cheŋ ka bɛ, ka wana ale soa duŋsaŋa de meena ale bo fi niŋ la?>
translated: He said to them, “If anyone would come after me, let him deny himself and take up his cross and follow me.

ref: Lamech and Zillah had a son named Tubal Cain who made tools out of bronze and iron. They also had a daughter, whose name was Naamah.
text: Zilla mɛ a jam biak ka nidoabili a te wa yuei ayen, Tubal Keen. Tubal Keen a jam ka choabiik. Wa jam ta ka toa, wa yuei ale Naama.
translated: The land where we live belongs to the people of the past, Tubal Keen. Tubal Keen belongs to the ancestors. We live on the land, we belong to Naama.

ref: Later, Judah chose Tamar as a wife for Er, his oldest son.
text: Juda jam yaali ka nipok a te Ɛri waai ate wa liŋ biak la. Nipowa yuei jam le Tamara.
translated: There was a certain woman who gave birth to a child and named him Eri. The woman's name was Tamara.

ref: Jacob, also known as Israel, traveled to the south of Eder Tower, where he set up camp.
text: Izirali a ŋman cheŋ wa niŋ nyiŋ a gaam Migdal Eder a ga ti wa viuku.
translated: Israel went and camped there, and they stayed at Migdal Eder where they had camped.

ref: Jacob and his family had left Bethel and were still a long way from Ephrath, when the time came for Rachel's baby to be born.
text: Ate ba cheŋ nyini Betel. Ba ale jam yalima maga a nyini Efrat la, Arekel yaa jam boro a yaa yen wa biak ate ku jam tua te wa.
translated: They buried Bethel. They buried him near the road that goes to Efrat, and Rachel died while giving birth to him, and they buried her there too.

ref: She had already hidden them in the cushion she used as a saddle and was sitting on it. Laban searched everywhere and did not find them.
text: Yɔgyɔgla Arekel yaa pai yenni wenaŋa a su ka wa laalaakomi dafieluku po a yaa kali ŋa zuk. Laban yaa gisi jaab meena ale bo viuku po la, alege wa an nya jaaboa.
translated: Arekel was very angry because his sister had gone to the farm and had not yet returned. Laban saw the goat in the goat pen, but he did not see the goat herder.

ref: Jacob said, “I have seen God face to face, and I am still alive.” So he named the place Peniel.
text: Ate Jekob yaa te jigini yuei ayen. Peniel, a weeni ayen, <<Ku le ka diila ale nna, m le Wen nya chaab kama alege ate jaab an nye m nyuvunni.
translated: Jacob's brother came to meet him. Peniel, seeing him, said, "Ku, let us go home; I have seen Wen's footprints, and he must have passed this way."

ref: Why don't you go on ahead and let me travel along slowly with the children, the herds, and the flocks. We can meet again in the country of Edom.”
text: Dila nyiŋ be dɛ niŋ a cheŋ m nyɔnɔ, ate mi fi tomteerɔwa a vi duŋsaŋa ŋaaŋ maga maga ale bisaŋa ate ti jam paari fu Seir tɛŋka, m dek nyɔnɔ.
translated: The people of the land took their livestock and set out on a journey, and they traveled through the wilderness for many days until they reached the border of Seir, and they settled there.

ref: I saw seven fat, healthy cows come up out of the river, and they began feeding on the grass.
text: Ate niiga ŋayopɔi a nyini benni po a jueli a biigi ale a nyaksi a bo wuutaŋa po a dɛ.
translated: One day a man went to the bush and killed an animal and brought it back to the village.

ref: That evening when Jacob came in from the fields, Leah told him, “You're sleeping with me tonight. I hired you with my son's love flowers.” They slept together that night,
text: Junɔinni ate Jekob a nyini talim a cheena la, Leya yaa cheŋ ga tu wa suik. Wa yaa weeni wa ayen, <<Fi magsi fi goa ale mu kama dii nyiŋla m pai ka m biika ŋanvaata a tuni fi nyiŋ.>> Ate wa sum goa ale wa yok kula.
translated: When Jacob saw that there was grain in Egypt, Leah said to her husband, "Go there and buy us some grain so that we and our children may live and not die." So the man went to buy grain.

ref: But God came to Abimelech in a dream and said, “You have taken a married woman into your home, and for this you will die!”
text: Alege Wen yaa jam Abimelek jigi gɔdaasima po yok kunyi a yaa weeni wa ayen, <<Fi a nye ase ka kpio la, dii nyiŋla nipowa ate fi ta la ka nipok waai ale yali.>>
translated: And the woman went to Abimelek and told him what had happened, saying, "He is the one who killed his people, and he has given us their wives and children to be our slaves."

ref: I will give you and your family all the land you can see. It will be theirs forever!
text: Tɛŋka meena ate fi a nya la m le pai te fi ale fi ŋaaŋbisaŋa taam wuu.
translated: The woman said that she had never seen such a beautiful thing before.

ref: Now I want you to promise in the name of God that you will always be loyal to me and my descendants, just as I have always been loyal to you in this land where you have lived as a foreigner.”
text: Yɔgyɔgla de pɛ Wen niŋ ayen fi kan nye venta nyeka ale mi yaase mi bisaŋa yaase m ŋaaŋsa. Sak mi ale tɛŋ kaai ate fi boro ase nichaanɔ la jiirim buui dek ate mi sak ale fu la.>>
translated: The story says that Wen did something to his mother that made her very angry, and she cursed him. So he went and stayed with his uncle, and later he came back and begged his mother for forgiveness.

ref: The Lord knew that Jacob loved Rachel more than he did Leah, and so he gave children to Leah, but not to Rachel.
text: Naawen ale nya ayen wa ka yaalika ale Leya la, Wa yaa nye ate Leya a biak alege ate Arekel a chim kirik.
translated: The woman said that she saw Leya going, and she said that Leya went to Arekel’s house.

ref: he said, “I now put a curse on Canaan! He will be the lowest slave of his brothers.
text: Wa yaa weeni ayen, <<Kikaasa va Keenan! Wa le chim ka yɔma te wa suataŋa.>>
translated: He said to them, "Get up, Keenan! Let us go, for the enemy is approaching us."

ref: and he told them: Your father isn't as friendly with me as he used to be, but the God my ancestors worshiped has been on my side.
text: Wa yaa weeni ba ayen, <<Mi nya kama ayen ni kowa nyeelaŋa ale mu a kan nye ase ku ale jam ka diila, alege m kowa Wenni a bo ale mu kama.
translated: He said to them, "I have told you that no one should take another's things, and if someone takes something, it is not good, and no one should go to Wen and take anything."

ref: Besides, she is my half sister. We have the same father, but different mothers.
text: A ŋman gum du, wa sum ka m toa, m kowa lie, wa daa m mawa lie ate wa yaa chim m pok.
translated: When I was a child, my mother gave me to my grandmother, and she raised me until I grew up.

ref: So the king told his men to let Abram and Sarai take their possessions and leave.
text: Faaro yaa weeni wa nuruma ate ba te ti Abram a cheŋ ale wa powa ale wa ŋantaŋa meena ate wa ta la.
translated: The next day Abraham got up early and took some food and a skin of water and gave them to Hagar. Then he sent her away with the boy.

ref: When Reuben returned to the well and did not find Joseph there, he tore his clothes in sorrow.
text: Aruben ale ŋman cheŋ vinni a nya ale Josefi karo la, wa yaa dari wa gattaŋa cheeri.
translated: Reuben saw a dream and told it to his brothers, and they hated him even more.

ref: The king's personal servant told Joseph, “In my dream I saw a vine
text: Ate daam teerɔ kpagni yaa weeni Josefi wa gɔdaasimaŋa. Wa weeni wa ayen, <<M gɔdaasimaŋa po m nya ka tiib m niŋ,
translated: Then Joseph's master took him and put him in prison. He said to him, "I am putting you in prison because I have found you guilty."

ref: He cooked the tasty food, brought it to his father, and said, “Father, please sit up and eat the meat I have brought you, so you can give me your blessing.”
text: Wa mɛ yaa nye ŋandiin tii ale masa a ta jam te wa kowa. A yaa weeni wa ayen, <<M koa yiti kali a dɛ m yaalimu ŋantaŋa, abe fi te mu fi niaka.>>
translated: They said that he had a dream in which he saw himself in a big town. He said to them, “I want to go and see my relatives there, and I will come back.”

ref: The snake was sneakier than any of the other wild animals that the Lord God had made. One day it came to the woman and asked, “Did God tell you not to eat fruit from any tree in the garden?”
text: Duŋsaŋa meena ate Naawen dii ale ka Wen la jam nye la po, waamu ale jam seba a gaam si meena. Bu yaa weeni nipowa ayen, <<Wen sum weeni kama ayen, <Ni kan dɛ tiisaŋa ale bo talimu po la yɔnaŋa?> >>
translated: The woman said that Naawen came and met Wen lying down, and she came and lay beside the woman. When she asked the woman, "Wen, why are you lying like this?" she said, "What made you come and lie on the bed with me?"

ref: I held the king's cup and squeezed the grapes into it, then I gave the cup to the king.”
text: Faaro beerika a jam bo ka m nisa po, mi yaa pai yɔnaŋa ŋmiak a nyo Faaro beerika po a te wa.>>
translated: Tomorrow morning at dawn when I go to the farm, I will see if the yams I planted have sprouted.

ref: Abraham separated seven female lambs from his flock of sheep,
text: Abrayam yaa gaari pasaa ŋayopɔi piisaŋa po,
translated: Abraham went to the market to buy some things.

ref: I will give you the land that I promised Abraham and Isaac, and it will belong to your family forever.
text: Tɛŋka ate m jam pai te Abrayam ale Aizik la, m le pai a te fi mɛ, ate m le pai te fi ŋaaŋbisa baai ale za ba nyini fi ŋaaŋnyiŋ a jam la.>>
translated: Abraham and Isaac went to the place that God had told him, and he took the wood for the burnt offering and laid it on his son Isaac.

ref: At this time the Canaanites and the Perizzites were still living in the same area, and so there wasn't enough pastureland left for Abram and Lot with all of their animals. Besides this, the men who took care of Abram's animals and the ones who took care of Lot's animals started quarreling.
text: Kpaliŋ ale jam bo baai ale a nya Abram duŋsaŋa zuk la ale baai ale a nya Lɔti duŋsaŋa zuk la suŋsuŋ. Keenan demma ale Perizzi demma mɛ jam maa bo ka tɛŋ kala dila po.
translated: The descendants of Japheth were also included among those who saw Abram’s greatness and those who saw Lot’s greatness in the same way. The Canaanites and the Perizzites were also living in the land at that time.

ref: We were out in the field, tying up bundles of wheat. Suddenly my bundle stood up, and your bundles gathered around and bowed down to it.”
text: Ti boro a bobi kpari ŋanta laŋka po ate mi kpari ŋantaŋa deri yiti zaani, alege ate nama bobaŋa a zaani gilim mi koori tɛŋ di niŋ.>>
translated: If you want to climb a tall tree, first climb a short one nearby to practice, then you can climb the tall one.

ref: Jacob set up a tombstone over her grave, and it is still there.
text: Ate Jekob a feri buntain ase zaŋi la wa boosuku zuk, a jam paari jinla tanni a za kama a teeri Arekel boosuku wari.
translated: Jacob's father got up early in the morning and took his son to school, and when they arrived there he left him with his teacher at the school.

ref: Also take seven pairs of every kind of bird with you. Do this so there will always be animals and birds on the earth.
text: ale nuinsa buuni siyopɔi, jaduok ale jasari ate si buuni a vua a bo tɛŋka meena.
translated: The child went to the farm, and the dog and the cat also went with him to the farm to play.

ref: Jacob said, “I have seen God face to face, and I am still alive.” So he named the place Peniel.
text: Ate Jekob yaa te jigini yuei ayen. Peniel, a weeni ayen, <<Ku le ka diila ale nna, m le Wen nya chaab kama alege ate jaab an nye m nyuvunni.
translated: Jacob said to him, "Please tell me your name." Peniel replied, "Why do you ask my name? I have shown you great kindness, yet you have not told me your name."

ref: About that time Judah left his brothers in the hill country and went to live near his friend Hirah in the town of Adullam.
text: Va ŋala po, Juda yaa basi wa suataŋa ale cheŋ a ga bo le nuru ate wa nyini Adullam tɛŋka. Wa yuei jam le Yira.
translated: When he fled, Judah left his garment beside her and fled outside, and went up to Adullam. He went and stayed with Hirah.

ref: Noah did everything God told him to do.
text: Nowa yaa nye dii meena ti Wen weeni wa la.
translated: Nowa has given birth to a girl and named her Wen weeni wa la.

ref: Abram went as far as the sacred tree of Moreh in a place called Shechem. The Canaanites were still living in the land at that time,
text: Abram jam cheŋ kama a ga paari More ti kpiemu ale bo Sikiim la. Dila po Keenan demma a jam bo tɛŋka po kama.
translated: Abram went up from there to the region of the Negev, and lived between Kadesh and Shur. For a while he stayed in Gerar.

ref: Lot went outside and shut the door behind him.
text: Lɔti yaa nyini a yik tuoku a kpari ale tu ba,
translated: The child is crying because his mother has gone to the farm.

ref: As Jacob continued on his way to the east,
text: Jekob yaa tom wa nyeerimu du a cheŋ ga paari nuru baai ale bo konni po la.
translated: Jacob went to the river to fetch water but did not find anyone there.

ref: I will give you more descendants than there are specks of dust on the earth, and someday it will be easier to count those specks of dust than to count your descendants.
text: M le basi ate fi ŋaaŋbisaŋa a piisi ase tintain busuŋ la, ate waai a dan baga a chiini tintain busuŋku, wa le baga a chiini fi ŋaaŋbisaŋa.
translated: And so they took the sheep and tied it in the middle of the road, and they placed its head on one side of the road and its legs on the other side of the road.

ref: and said, “Please come to my home where I can serve you.
text: Abrayam yaa weeni ayen, <<M nyɔnɔ ni dan nya ku magsi ayen ni jam m yenni, ni kan gaam ni yɔmawa taamu.
translated: Abraham said to them, "I am a stranger and a sojourner among you; give me a burial site among you."

ref: One day, Sarah noticed Hagar's son Ishmael playing, and she said to Abraham, “Get rid of that Egyptian slave woman and her son! I don't want him to inherit anything. It should all go to my son.”
text: wa yaa weeni Abrayam ayen, <<Basi ate nipok yɔmawa ale wa nidoabinni a cheŋ, nipok yɔma wala nidoabinni a kan maa ta charuk ale mi nidoabinni Aizik.>>
translated: And Abraham said to them, “So if the girl is not willing to come with you, you will be free from this oath of mine; only do not take my son Isaac back there.”

ref: The land where he camped was owned by the descendants of Hamor, the father of Shechem. So Jacob paid them
text: Jekob a jam da tɛŋka ka kpana kook a nyini, Sikiim biika Ayaamo bisaŋa jigi, tɛŋ kala zuk ate wa jam ti wa viuku.
translated: Jacob was a quiet man who stayed at home, while Sikiim's child Ayaamo went out every day, and the whole town was quiet and peaceful.

ref: However, I have heard what you asked me to do for Ishmael, and so I will also bless him with many descendants. He will be the father of twelve princes, and I will make his family a great nation.
text: Alege Ismael jigi, m wom fu kama. M le sum niak wa. M le basi ate wa ta kiri abe m basi ate wa ŋaaŋbisaŋa piisi. Wa le chim kpaga pi ale ŋaye ko abe m basi ate wa chim tɛŋ kpieŋ.
translated: Ismael went to the farm, he went to work. He went and met a lion. He ran away and left his hoe there and ran away and left his cutlass there too. He climbed a tree and the lion came and stood under the tree and he climbed down and killed it.
